commands.review_summary:
This PR makes `buildGroupChatContext` render `channel` for `ChatType: "channel"` and adds regression coverage for Mattermost channel versus group wording.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +17. Total +18 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: Mattermost public channels map to `ChatType: "channel"`, `get-reply-run` sends channel turns through `buildGroupChatContext`, and current main emits the group-chat intro. I did not run a live Mattermost server repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is contributor-provided real behavior proof plus normal PR merge gates, not a code repair ClawSweeper can generate.

target.title: fix(groups): use 'channel' wording in intro for ChatType channel (fixes #95645) (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

For any non-DM Mattermost conversation — including a public channel — the system-prompt intro declares *"You are in a `<provider>` group chat."* even when `ChatType` is `"channel"`. This contradicts the authoritative inbound-meta block which reports `chat_type: "channel"`, causing agents to misidentify public channels as group chats and emit the wrong `chat_type` in tool arguments.

## What does this PR do?

Uses the actual `ChatType` value to pick `"channel"` or `"group chat"` in the intro line rendered by `buildGroupChatContext`.

## Related Issue

Fixes #95645

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)
- [x] AI-assisted (Hermes Agent)

## Changes Made

- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts`: Added `chatTypeLabel` ternary that renders "channel" when `ChatType === "channel"`, "group chat" otherwise. Interpolated into the intro line.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts`: Added test verifying correct wording for both `ChatType: "channel"` and `ChatType: "group"`.

## How to Test

1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts` — all 7 verifications pass
2. `grep -n 'chatTypeLabel' src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts` — confirms the ternary at line 242

## Evidence

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The intro prose in `buildGroupChatContext` unconditionally rendered "group chat" for all non-DM conversations, including public channels where `ChatType` is `"channel"`.
- **Real environment tested:** macOS, Node 22, upstream/main at `e9be15ff`.
- **Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts
 ✓  auto-reply  src/auto-reply/reply/groups.test.ts (7 tests) 111ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  7 passed (7)
```
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n 'chatTypeLabel' src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts
242:  const chatTypeLabel = params.sessionCtx.ChatType === "channel" ? "channel" : "group chat";
244:  lines.push(`You are in a ${providerLabel} ${chatTypeLabel}.`);

$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const s=fs.readFileSync('src/auto-reply/reply/groups.ts','utf8'); console.log('Has ChatType ternary:', s.includes('ChatType === \"channel\" ? \"channel\" : \"group chat\"')); console.log('Has interpolation:', s.includes('\${chatTypeLabel}'));"
Has ChatType ternary: true
Has interpolation: true
```
- **Observed result after fix:** When `ChatType` is `"channel"`, the intro now renders *"You are in a Mattermost channel."* instead of *"You are in a Mattermost group chat."*. When `ChatType` is `"group"`, it still renders *"You are in a Mattermost group chat."* as before.
- **What was not tested:** Live Mattermost channel interaction (the change is a string interpolation in a pure function; the verification suite covers both code paths).

## Checklist

- [x] Read Contributing Guide
- [x] Conventional Commits
- [x] Searched for duplicates
- [x] Only related changes
- [x] Verification pass
- [x] Added regression coverage
- [x] Did NOT edit CHANGELOG.md
- [x] AI-assisted disclosure included
